author: sweet_milky

title: The Best Workout App in the World
content: Update, June 24, 2026 - I’ve been using this app for a while and I like it even more than when I started.  One of the rare and fantastic things about the workouts is that they are genuinely well thought out, really nicely put together.  It seems a person sat down and thought this through and put the exercises in the right order and the most beneficial exercises.  So, there are no surprises.  The workouts do what they say they will do.  Anyway, I do wish they’d put out some new PROGRAMS, though I’m sure at some point they will.   ———-         I am absolutely crazy about this app.  It has everything, almost (no tai chi, yet).  All types of exercises and yoga . . . Of various lengths.  I am on a 30 day dumbbell program right now.  The variety of exercises in that one program is amazing.  I’ve never seen some of those moves.  Also, doing a 7-minute workout in the morning; love those unique moves, too.  Basically, you get this and you don’t need anything else.  I downloaded dozens of workout apps and this is the one who stood out way, way above the others; nothing even compares. Totally love it!

title: Simply great, plus comments
content:
I love this app. So convenient, and helps me just get to the exercise without having to do any research or get distracted by other things (which happens when I go on YT for routines). I exercise with way more variety and frequency because of it. As someone who is trying to get back into running after a long hiatus, it is very helpful for strength/cross training. Plus there are so many options! I have favorites, but also just the daily or randomly choosing another routine keeps it fresh.

My only comment is that I wish there were some more options for the sound indicators. The voice and bell are fine, but it would be great to have something less wordy or just different, more subtle sounds. I often listen to my own music or podcasts while I do the workouts and I would love some auditory ques that weren’t so distracting.

title: Perfect
content: So far it’s amazing and the work outs are better than I could have imagined.  I love the different variations and added “spice/movements” they’ve added to main exercises we all know from working out prior to this app.  They give you more bang for your buck especially in endurance and mobility, plus your body warms up perfectly with no stress added to joints or anything else that could cause injury during your workout. 🦄🦄🦄🤓
author: SwanAna41

title: Love it, but…
content:
This app has been a huge help keeping me focused and motivated to workout right from home. It’s convenient and easy to follow. So many options for what your goals are.
Only downside is there is a lack of pregnancy safe strength training, mobility and flexibility workouts. There are stretches and postpartum workouts, but not much for during the pregnancy. Much less for breakdowns of the different trimesters. 
Considering this is a women’s fitness app, I believe there should be more resources for the pregnancy stage that keep mom healthy and baby safe.
Please consider for a near future update.

author: Vovó MC

title: Why is there such a string focus on losing weight?
content:
I used to use this app for a while and it worked okay, but stopped working out and tried to redownload it.

Why. Is. There. Such. A. Strong. Emphasis. On. Weight. Loss!?

You can’t even log in and use the app without setting a weight loss goal! Every single exercise comes with a note on how much weight you’ll lose. I understand it’s useful if you ARE trying to lose weight, but the app SPECIFICALLY ASKS what your goals are (disappointed to find out that increasing flexibility wasn’t an option, but I guess it’s a little niche) and never once did I include weight loss. 

I think it’s toxic (and this is more critique of diet culture as whole rather than the app itself) to assume every goddamn person tying to stay fit is trying to lose weight. I just want to be healthy, not feel pressured into losing weight when I’m already at a healthy one.

And, in the end, I don’t particularly care too much, I just deleted the app again, but what about the teenagers using this app? (There is a specific option when you log in to state you are under 18.) I don’t think it’s healthy to subject teenagers to that constant pressure, or worse, someone who has already struggled with EDs or an obsession with weight loss/appearance.

It’s one thing for the option to be there, in fact, I think it’s great for those who need or want it, but the way that this is constantly shoved down your throat is ridiculous. The creators are (or at should be) fully aware that losing weight isn’t the only reason to work out, so why don’t they act like it?

title: Very useful!

The workouts progressively get more difficult, but are enjoyable, making it difficult to even wish to quit. This app does wonders and fits many ages, even younger people who struggle to stay motivated! Highly recommended this!

The workouts do feel a little stupid to do and I feel like I look stupid doing them but I am feeling and looking better day by day! The music that is provided is nice so I don’t have to overlap another app to play music (and you can skip songs) wish there was more of a variety of songs though. All things considered though I recommend this app to people who don’t feel comfortable going to the gym or can’t afford a trainer!

title: Can’t get out of ads

content: I only signed up for the free trial TODAY and was charged at the moment for a monthly subscription I never wanted. This feels misleading and unfair.

title: Women’s workout
content:
It is a great workout, not to intense for me. Just turned 60 Update I’m 61 and still love it. I'm 63 this time around love everything but the price ( used to be free). just saying I’m 63 now and need to get in shape.

Now I’m 64 recently had total knee replacement. Today is my fourth straight day and I love it.

title: False claims of free trial
content: App falsely claims to have a free trial  but was charged immediately. Apple Support will not refund the charge despite the app saying I was signing up for a free trial. Extremely unhappy with the situation.

title: Love it
content: It’s great! Can be hard if it’s been a while. Very fast paced depending on what you choose. I been doing gym and nothing was working. This works! Moves look easy but they work for women! If you wanna lose weight or tone up at home this is a great app!

title: Love it
content: I love this app I just wished it showed how to do it more loved it though I recommend it
author: Nova_shining star

title: good app but ruining it with ads
content: i’ve always been off and on with this app for years and i kept coming back to it bc it offered good workouts for free with no ads and now you have to watch a 90 second ad before EVERY workout unless you’d rather pay for premium. paying for subscriptions isn’t something i can just afford so i’m personally extremely disappointed they decided to add ads to the app.
author: akjsksksk

title: Not great anymore
content: Too many adds 90 seconds??? Too much.
author: Carole18

title: Love this app
content: When I retired I wanted to do nothing but quickly realized that’s not an option. You must move your 68 year old body. I do the daily 8 minute workout and it’s been a literal life saver. I’ve stretched the 8 minute work out to 35 minutes daily by adding my own program. I’m on the advanced level which took 7 months and I’ve lost 16 pounds and kept them off. I don’t do plank as it’s damaging to older wrists, elbows, shoulders and knees but that’s my only complaint. I’m grateful to have found this app.
author: LeinLv

title: it’s okish
content: the workouts are good but i don’t want to have to pay to do a workout or watch a ad
author: livy22!!

title: FIX THE ADS
content: Tbh I always loved this app and have used it for years but recently I actually deleted it a couple of times because of the add problem. I get it y’all wanna make money with no add subscriptions but to make it so we have to watch a three minute add before every. Single. Work out. Is completely unreasonable and very apparent and annoying it makes the whole app feel like a scam and a money grab. It becomes a huge de motivator and leads to me just exiting out of the whole app instead and doing a workout I already know.
author: Sativa,W

title: Love it!
content: Awesome app! I’ve been with it over a year. It’s worth the money and you can either workout a little or workout more. The choice are great!

title: Fabulous app!
content: Recommend to all women.

title: I love it
content: I’m 12 and I have never found a app that actually makes you muscle feel strong and I’m glad it tells you when your most likely to reach your goal it gives me a drive to do it everyday even if I have school and I hope that I get close to my goal

title: Players
content: Excellent
author: d11278

title: Great workouts; data won’t load to new devices
content: Love the workouts. Cheaper than a gym membership and worth it. Love that I can link Spotify. I do the daily plan and two reps of the daily 7. However, just got a new phone and none of the data loaded to my new device, even with my log in. I’ve lost a lot of tracked progress which sucks. Also got an Apple Watch series 11 and cannot figure out how to get the watch to register my work outs as exercise. Was using a series 3 and that worked fine. Can’t seem to confirm if there’s a compatibility issue. Google hasn’t helped. Anyway, starting at square one again. 😭
author: Devvy Poo

title: Do it
content: Get the app. Do what you can and you will see results.
author: Bayview603

title: 0-5 do not recommend! I gave it five so you can see!!!
content: I wanted to be able to work out at home without having to come up with a routine so i downloaded this app. But every time i try to get my workout in it’s always buy premium or watch a 90 second add. This app in my opinion is a 0/5 stars and i do not recommend. Hope this helps you with making a decision for your fitness app or goals!

title: Not free
content: Says it’s free, but it makes you go through a ton of steps, then gives you no way to continue without paying $40/yr.

title: AI slop now
content: i was a paying member of this app for years and will not be using this app anymore. most of the workouts are just AI slop now. the work outs themselves are barely even work outs, the guiding voice is telling you to do one thing while the AI diagram is doing another. such a disappointment.
